messages.content:
Your goal is to explain an agent-based model. Your explanation must include the context of the model, its goals, and key parameters. For each parameter, include the range of values (if provided) and the value that we have set. Do not write any summary or conclusion. We have 16 parameters:
  - memory-lifetime, from 1.0 to 10.0. We set it to 5.
  - alt-health-mean-initial, from 1.0 to 3.0. We set it to 1.69.
  - habit-threshold, from 1.0 to 10.0. We set it to 2.
  - p-interact, from 0.0 to 1.0. We set it to 0.3.
  - social-susceptibility, from 0.0 to 1.0. We set it to 0.2.
  - incumbent-initial-habit, from 0.0 to 10.0. We set it to 10.
  - alt-env-mean-initial, from 0.0 to 3.0. We set it to 1.12.
  - social-blindness, from 0.0 to 1.0. We set it to 0.51.
  - justification, from 0.0 to 1.0. We set it to 0.61.
  - cognitive-dissonance-threshold, from 0.0 to 1.0. We set it to 0.15.
  - network-parameter, from 2.0 to 10.0. We set it to 8.
  - number-of-agents. We set it to 1000.
  - habit-on?. We set it to True.
  - network-type. We set it to "watts-strogatz".
  - norms. We set it to True.
  - social-conformity. We set it to 0.28.


The context and goals of the model are as follows. The overall objective of the agent-based model is to reproduce adoption behaviours of milk consumption by the British public (1974-2005), by replicating individual preferences and decision factors. Specifically, the goal of the model is to explore the influence of perception, habits and social influence in an individual’s decision-making process of milk choice. The model looks to replicate the substitution of whole milk for skimmed and semi-skimmed milk from the 1970s onwards. The main outcome reported here is the average weekly consumption of each milk type per person. The simulation uses both a theoretical grounding and empirical data to inform the ABM, with calibration performed against observed macro level data. 

In particular, the study conducts an experiment to compare the performance of two model variants in reproducing overserved milk consumption trends. These variants present different mechanisms for how agents become disposed to consider their choices, representing a threshold based, and a probability-based approach.
Decision-making follows a basic structure of: agent perception of choice characteristics, the triggering, or not, of disposition to consider alternatives, a set of scored choice functions made up of the perceived characteristics and modulated by habit and social influence, and finally, choice evaluation where agents consider the impact of their choices and may adjust their future decisions.

The agents in the model represent adult consumers who occupy a random position in an information environment. Each agent has a disposition to consider alternative milk choices. Two disposition mechanisms are tested in the model, a threshold-based approach, and a probability-based approach. Each agent makes a choice of milk selection based on a function for each alternative, made up of the perceived health and environmental characterises of each choice. These are computed at the initialisation of the simulation and then calculated at each time step. An agent’s milk choice function is modified by habit, social influence, and evaluation of previous choices. Agents ascribe different relative importance to each constituent part of the choice function (health factors and environmental factors). If the disposition requirement has been met, consumption of each milk type is split proportionately by the size of each choice function, modulated by the other influences. If not, agents keep their existing choice.

Agents (n=1,000) start with an existing choice based on the dominant position of whole milk versus skimmed varieties in 1974 (start year of the data). All agents are part of a social network. Each agent in the network can sense and be influenced by the choice function of each milk alternative for other agents in their network. Links between agents are unidirectional, and influence occurs as a function of interaction probability, with the degree of influence characterised by agent susceptibility. Social norms are globally perceived by agents and impact the weightings of the choice function
Setup and Go.

Monitors:

Two monitors track the number of turtles that have consume mostly whole-milk (incumbent initial choice) or skimmed/semi-skimmed milk (alternative initial choice).

Plots:

One plot shows the percentage of majority whole milk or skimmed/semi-skimmed milk consumers.

The second plots the average consumption of each milk type over time. This is the data that the calibration exercise uses to try and replicate observed consmption data.

Parameters:

12 paraemters that governs whether an agent will consider changing its milk type, and how much of each milk type an agent will consume. We refer viewers to the manuscript based on this model for further details.
The appearance of a crossover in the types of milk consumed and the shape of the curves over time.
(a reference to the model's URL on the web if it has one, as well as any other necessary credits, citations, and links)

ollama_format.description: Structured smoke output schema for one text response.
ollama_format.title: StructuredSmokeText

structured_output_schema.description: Structured smoke output schema for one text response.
structured_output_schema.title: StructuredSmokeText

parsed_response.response_text:
This agent-based model simulates the adoption behaviors of milk consumption among the British public from 1974 to 2005, replicating individual preferences and decision factors underlying the historical transition from whole milk to skimmed and semi-skimmed varieties. The simulation integrates theoretical frameworks with empirical data, employing calibration against observed macro-level consumption patterns to validate outcomes. Agents represent adult consumers positioned randomly within an information environment, each starting with milk choices reflecting the 1974 market dominance of whole milk versus skimmed varieties. All 1,000 agents exist within a unidirectional social network based on the Watts-Strogatz topology, where they can sense and influence other agents' choice functions through interaction probabilities.

The primary objective is to explore how perception, habits, and social influence interact within individual decision-making processes regarding milk choice, with the main outcome metric being average weekly consumption per person for each milk type. The study compares two model variants employing different disposition mechanisms for triggering consideration of alternatives: a threshold-based approach versus a probability-based approach. Decision-making follows a structured sequence involving perception of health and environmental choice characteristics, disposition triggering, scored choice functions weighted by habit strength and social influence, and post-choice evaluation where agents may adjust future decisions. Social norms operate globally to impact choice function weightings, while habit formation and cognitive dissonance thresholds modify consumption patterns over time.

The model utilizes 16 parameters governing agent behavior and network characteristics. memory-lifetime ranges from 1.0 to 10.0 and is set to 5. alt-health-mean-initial ranges from 1.0 to 3.0 and is set to 1.69. habit-threshold ranges from 1.0 to 10.0 and is set to 2. p-interact ranges from 0.0 to 1.0 and is set to 0.3. social-susceptibility ranges from 0.0 to 1.0 and is set to 0.2. incumbent-initial-habit ranges from 0.0 to 10.0 and is set to 10. alt-env-mean-initial ranges from 0.0 to 3.0 and is set to 1.12. social-blindness ranges from 0.0 to 1.0 and is set to 0.51. justification ranges from 0.0 to 1.0 and is set to 0.61. cognitive-dissonance-threshold ranges from 0.0 to 1.0 and is set to 0.15. network-parameter ranges from 2.0 to 10.0 and is set to 8. number-of-agents is set to 1000. habit-on? is set to True. network-type is set to "watts-strogatz". norms is set to True. social-conformity is set to 0.28.

message.reasoning:
The user wants an explanation of an agent-based model in a specific JSON format. The explanation must include:
1. Context of the model
2. Goals of the model  
3. Key parameters (all 16 of them) with ranges and set values
4. No summary or conclusion

The schema requires a single field "response_text" containing the explanation as a string.

Let me organize the information:

Context:
- Reproduce adoption behaviours of milk consumption by British public (1974-2005)
- Replicate individual preferences and decision factors
- Explore influence of perception, habits and social influence in milk choice decision-making
- Replicate substitution of whole milk for skimmed/semi-skimmed milk from 1970s onwards
- Uses theoretical grounding and empirical data
- Calibration against observed macro level data

Goals:
- Compare performance of two model variants (threshold-based vs probability-based disposition mechanisms)
- Main outcome: average weekly consumption of each milk type per person

Parameters (16 total):
1. memory-lifetime: 1.0 to 10.0, set to 5
2. alt-health-mean-initial: 1.0 to 3.0, set to 1.69
3. habit-threshold: 1.0 to 10.0, set to 2
4. p-interact: 0.0 to 1.0, set to 0.3
5. social-susceptibility: 0.0 to 1.0, set to 0.2
6. incumbent-initial-habit: 0.0 to 10.0, set to 10
7. alt-env-mean-initial: 0.0 to 3.0, set to 1.12
8. social-blindness: 0.0 to 1.0, set to 0.51
9. justification: 0.0 to 1.0, set to 0.61
10. cognitive-dissonance-threshold: 0.0 to 1.0, set to 0.15
11. network-parameter: 2.0 to 10.0, set to 8
12. number-of-agents: set to 1000 (no range mentioned)
13. habit-on?: set to True (boolean)
14. network-type: set to "watts-strogatz" (string)
15. norms: set to True (boolean)
16. social-conformity: set to 0.28 (no range mentioned, but I should check context - seems to be 0.0-1.0 based on similar params, but user didn't specify range so I'll just state the value)

Wait, looking at the user's list, they said "We have 16 parameters" and listed them. For number-of-agents, network-type, habit-on?, and norms, they didn't provide ranges, so I should just mention the set values without making up ranges.

Structure of the response:
- Context paragraph
- Goals paragraph  
- Parameters section listing all 16 with ranges (where applicable) and set values

No summary or conclusion allowed.

The response must be valid JSON with a single key "response_text" containing the text.

Let me draft the content:

Context: This agent-based model simulates the adoption behaviors of milk consumption among the British public from 1974 to 2005. It aims to replicate individual preferences and decision factors underlying the historical substitution of whole milk for skimmed and semi-skimmed varieties beginning in the 1970s. The simulation integrates theoretical frameworks with empirical data, employing calibration against observed macro-level consumption patterns to validate outcomes. Agents represent adult consumers positioned randomly within an information environment, each making milk selections based on perceived health and environmental characteristics, modified by habit formation and social influence.

Goals: The primary objective is to explore how perception, habits, and social influence interact within individual decision-making processes regarding milk choice. The model specifically seeks to reproduce observed trends in milk consumption, with the main outcome metric being average weekly consumption per person for each milk type. The study conducts comparative experiments between two model variants that employ different mechanisms for triggering agent disposition to consider alternatives: a threshold-based approach versus a probability-based approach. Decision-making follows a structured process involving perception of choice characteristics, disposition triggering, scored choice functions (incorporating health and environmental factors weighted by habit and social influence), and post-choice evaluation where agents may adjust future decisions based on outcomes.

Parameters: The model operates with 16 parameters. memory-lifetime ranges from 1.0 to 10.0 and is set to 5. alt-health-mean-initial ranges from 1.0 to 3.0 and is set to 1.69. habit-threshold ranges from 1.0 to 10.0 and is set to 2. p-interact ranges from 0.0 to 1.0 and is set to 0.3. social-susceptibility ranges from 0.0 to 1.0 and is set to 0.2. incumbent-initial-habit ranges from 0.0 to 10.0 and is set to 10. alt-env-mean-initial ranges from 0.0 to 3.0 and is set to 1.12. social-blindness ranges from 0.0 to 1.0 and is set to 0.51. justification ranges from 0.0 to 1.0 and is set to 0.61. cognitive-dissonance-threshold ranges from 0.0 to 1.0 and is set to 0.15. network-parameter ranges from 2.0 to 10.0 and is set to 8. number-of-agents is set to 1000. habit-on? is set to True. network-type is set to "watts-strogatz". norms is set to True. social-conformity is set to 0.28.

7. alt-env-mean-initial - check

Now format as JSON:
{"response_text": "Context paragraph\n\nGoals paragraph\n\nParameters paragraph"}

The text should be a single string with appropriate paragraph breaks.

One final check on parameter details:

- habit-threshold: range 1.0-10.0, value 2
- p-interact: range 0.0-1.0, value 0.3
- social-susceptibility: range 0.0-1.0, value 0.2
